title: arum orientale subsp. amoenum
intro: Arum orientale subsp. amoenum is a subspecies of the Arum orientale plant, commonly known as the 'Cretan Arum' or 'Arum of Crete', which is a flowering perennial plant native to the Mediterranean region. The plant is rich in medicinal properties, particularly in its rhizomes, which contain compounds such as alkaloids and glycosides that have been used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Arum orientale subsp. amoenum include its potential to reduce inflammation, ease pain, and act as an antispasmodic agent, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. Herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to create teas, ointments, and salves for topical and internal use.
